chapter_context.summary: The Ark brings plagues, tumors, and panic to the Philistine cities, and shatters the idol of Dagon in his own temple.

outline.text: The Ark in Ashdod; Dagon Falls Before the Ark
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Ark in Ashdod; Dagon Falls Before the Ark (5:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

outline.text: The Hand of the LORD Heavy Upon Gath and Ekron
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Hand of the LORD Heavy Upon Gath and Ekron (5:6-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

key_themes.title: Sovereignty of God
key_themes.description: God raises up the humble (Hannah, Samuel, David) and humbles the proud (Eli's sons, Saul).

key_themes.title: The Heart vs. Outward Appearance
key_themes.description: God looks at the inner spiritual character, not physical stature or social status.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 5, how does this encourage you to trust God when circumstances look bleak?
How does this chapter prompt you to examine the sincerity of your own heart before the LORD?

cultural_historical.title: Transition to Monarchy
cultural_historical.text: Israel's request for a king represents a major cultural shift from a tribal league led by temporary judges to a centralized dynastic monarchy.
